H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78 (strong confidence). Qualitative (n=29) reports 66% notice improvement, settle ~10 minutes, music 'Loved it' (10/29), rhythm 'Very settling / calming' (8/29). YouTube views=30 — highest across HEALING, but retention data absent. AutoGluon predicts 6.859, slightly below observed — a small gap, but a signal of overperformance. The model cross-buckets H-OS-L1 to X-ME-L1 and X-ME-L2. The distribution peaks at 8 (26), with a strong right tail. Consistent with being the strongest over-stimulation DESCENT.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This track cuts through sensory overload with clarity, helping many find calm again.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73 (strong confidence). Qualitative (n=21) reports 'Noticeable improvement' (10/21), settle time at 10 minutes or immediate, music opinion split (10 liked, 6 neutral). The distribution is flatter than L1 with a smaller peak. YouTube views=5. Panel divided: one analyzer sees groove as borderline, another notes faster settling. No AutoGluon signal. Consistent with a reliable but less peak-performing over-stimulation tool.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: For overstimulation, this offers a balanced, gentle release that many find stabilizing.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=23) shows 8/23 use it while trying to sleep — a functional migration. Music and rhythm are both highly liked, with 10/23 reporting 'Very settling / calming.' Distribution tight, peak at 7. The protocol is analytically distinct (lowest mean pairwise similarity in clusters). The data suggests a strong candidate for a sleep-specific variant. No AutoGluon signal. Panel divided on whether this is a use-case shift or design flaw.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: A refined tool for sensory overload, it brings quiet focus, especially useful before sleep.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93 (strong confidence). Qualitative data (n=23) shows 63% report moderate to significant improvement, settle within 5–10 minutes, and rate music as balanced or very calming. YouTube views are minimal (7), suggesting limited audience reach. AutoGluon predicts a higher anxiety zone score (7.838), indicating underperformance relative to design signature. The distribution peaks at 8 and shows a right tail, consistent with high but not universal impact. The pattern is consistent with a strong entry-level anxiety DESCENT, though not yet achieving model-predicted peak.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: A calming track that helps many anxious listeners decompress, with most feeling noticeably better after a short settling period.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative feedback (n=20) reports moderate improvement and settle times clustering at 5 and 20 minutes, suggesting mixed entrainment speed. Music opinions are mixed, with 5/20 neutral. YouTube views=6. No AutoGluon prediction listed, but the response pattern is consistent with a stable but less impactful variant of the anxiety sequence. Distribution is tighter than L1 (1.62 vs 1.93), indicating predictable, middle-range benefit. Panel divided on whether L2 is a groove effect or structural underperformance.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A gentle way to ease anxiety, offering steady relief without dramatic changes — ideal for daily resilience.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative data (n=20) reports noticeable improvement, with a settle time mode of 'About 10 minutes' and rhythm rated as 'Very settling / calming' (10/20). The distribution is broad, peaking at 7 and 8 with no low-end outliers, suggesting high tolerability. YouTube views=4. No AutoGluon signal. The protocol is used in therapy and caregiving contexts (6/20), which may inflate variance. Consistent with a balanced mid-tier anxiety DESCENT.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: A consistent ally for stress, this track gently guides listeners toward a calmer, clearer state.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=24) shows 8/24 report 'Significant improvement' — the highest absolute count in any crisis protocol — and settle time is fast (5 minutes). However, the outcome distribution has a sharp ceiling at score 8, with no 9s or 10s, suggesting scale compression. AutoGluon predicts a higher crisis zone score (7.955), but no discrepancy is flagged. The high proportion of proxy raters (11/24) may explain the conservative scoring. Expected for crisis entry points.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In a moment of crisis, this track brings quick relief, helping many feel steadier within minutes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=16) reports mixed improvement, settle time mode 'About 10 minutes,' and music rating split. The distribution is slightly right-shifted, but pct_pos=55% exceeds L1 despite a higher mean. AutoGluon predicts the highest crisis score (7.955), over 1.4 points above observed — a material gap. The model flags H-CR-L2 as the highest-predicted entry, but real-world delivery lags. Source split (adviser-driven=3/33) may contribute — warrants further investigation.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: This protocol helps steady the ship during acute distress, offering reliable relief for most users.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=19) shows rapid settle time ('Immediately' at 7/19) and mixed music sentiment, including 'Not liked' at 4/19 — the only crisis protocol with explicit dislike. Distribution includes three 0s, indicating non-response. YouTube views=5. The cross-run drift is notable (sim=0.711). The protocol performs better than H-CR-L1 in mean but not in distribution. Suggests early entrainment may not sustain. Panel divided on whether this is a groove defect or population mismatch.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Designed for those in deep distress, it helps some settle quickly and find a sense of calm.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86 (strong confidence). Qualitative (n=15) reports 'Noticeable improvement' (6/15), settle time fast (~5 minutes), music 'Liked it' (10/15). The distribution peaks at 9 (11) and 8 (9), with a tail to 10 (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.539, above observed — a gap. Cross-buckets to X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2. The protocol has high cross-run drift (0.530), indicating evolving framing. Consistent with a top-tier sleep DESCENT with high listener engagement.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: A powerful sleep aid, this track helps many fall asleep faster and feel more refreshed.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=15) shows 11/15 are proxy raters (client/patient or someone they support), settle time mode 'About 20 minutes,' and 3/15 report 'Too busy' rhythm. Distribution peak at 7 (13), clean and tight. YouTube views=5. The high proxy ratio and 'Too busy' signal suggest it may be less effective for autonomous use. Panel divided on whether this indicates clinical utility or design flaw.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: A subtle but effective choice for rest, this protocol promotes relaxation with gentle cues.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=22) reports settle time 'About 5 minutes' (8/22) and rhythm 'Slightly busy' (7/22) — the highest 'busy' signal in the tier. Distribution tight, peak at 7 (6), then 8 (7). YouTube views=3. The tension between high scores and 'busy' rhythm warrants monitoring. AutoGluon no signal. The protocol is viable but may benefit from groove adaptation.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: A quiet companion for winding down, most users feel its calming effect by the end.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=23) reports 9/23 'Slightly busy' rhythm, the worst in THRIVING. Distribution bimodal, peaking at 7 (12) and tailing at 4 (6). AutoGluon no signal on rhythm feature. Panel divided: one model sees rhythm as overwhelming, another finds it consistent with focus design.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: A focused tool for clarity, it helps steady the mind and sharpen attention.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=17) shows 9/17 'About 10 minutes' for settle time. The distribution peaks at 8 (10) — the strongest in the series. Music opinion split (8 'Liked,' 5 'Loved'), rhythm 'Balanced' (8). The protocol shows the largest L1→L2 jump in THRIVING. Panel divided on whether this is a feature gradient or groove effect.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: This track helps you lock in, especially when concentration is vital for the task.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=29) shows 11/29 'Significant improvement,' 7 'Noticeable.' The distribution is bimodal: peaks at 7 (7) and 10 (5). YouTube views=15. The protocol has the highest mean but also highest variance. AutoGluon predicts 7.728. Panel deeply divided: one sees this as a success of the HOLD gradient, another sees it as a responder/non-responder split.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: For deep focus, this protocol enhances mental clarity and keeps distractions at bay.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=21) shows 8/21 reported 'Slight improvement' — the highest proportion of 'Slight' in the library. Settle time: 6/21 'Immediately,' suggesting immediate entrainment. Music opinion mostly positive. The distribution is flat, with no clear high-end tail. AutoGluon no feature signal for 'move' (HOLD architecture). Panel divided: some see static HOLD as underperforming, others note it's effective for immediate access.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: For creative work, this track gently clears mental blocks and invites flow.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=15) shows 6/15 'Loved it' for music — the strongest reception in the family. Change ratings improve (5 'Significant'). The distribution is narrow, peak at 8 (8). YouTube views=2. The protocol shows the clearest groove effect in the series. Panel divided on whether this is a sound design win or a content mismatch.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: As your creative session begins, this track helps sharpen focus and spark ideas.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32 (developing). Qualitative (n=25) shows 7/25 'Significant improvement,' 6 'Moderate,' and 5 'Noticeable.' The distribution is steep-front-loaded: 17/18 responses at 7–10. YouTube views=7. The protocol is the highest in Creative Flow but used in sleep contexts (5/25), suggesting functional overreach. Panel divided on whether this is a design strength or misalignment.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: At peak creative moments, this track amplifies clarity and unlocks deeper expression.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=23) reports 9/23 'About 20 minutes' settle time — the longest in THRIVING. Music opinions split. Distribution is balanced: 7 at 7 and 8. No AutoGluon signal. Panel divided: some see this as intentional, others see it as slow for maintenance.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: A consistent anchor, this protocol helps maintain balance through daily challenges.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=16) shows 7/16 'Slightly busy' rhythm — same as T-FC-L1. The distribution is messy, flat, with a 0-score. YouTube views=4. The protocol is the weakest in the series. Panel divided on whether this is a design fail or cohort mismatch.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: A steady presence, it offers support when balance feels out of reach.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73 (developing). Qualitative (n=19) shows 4/19 'Did NOT settle' — the strongest failure-to-settle signal in THRIVING. Music opinion mixed. The protocol is analytically isolated. Panel divided: one model sees this as a population split, another sees it as a usability flaw.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: This track gently brings clarity to mental fog, grounding you when you need it.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=22) shows 6 'About 20 minutes' settle time, 6 'Immediately.' Music: 10 'Loved,' 6 'Neutral.' AutoGluon predicts 8.230 — the highest of all zones, over 1.4 points above observed. Cross-buckets to anxiety, crisis, low, and focus. The protocol is the top cross-zone signal. Panel divided: one sees this as a meta-protocol, another sees it as underperforming.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: This track lifts mood and energy, helping some feel more alive and present.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=15) shows bimodal settle time: 4 'About 20 minutes,' 4 'Immediately.' Music: 7 'Loved.' AutoGluon predicts 8.230 — same as X-ME-L1. Cross-buckets to anxiety, crisis, low, and focus. The model sees this as a top-tier protocol despite modest outcome. Panel divided: one sees it as a strong design, another sees it as a signal gap.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: A gentle boost in energy and mood, it supports those needing a quiet lift.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 (developing). Qualitative (n=25) shows 5 'No effect,' 3 'Slight improvement.' The distribution shows a flat peak at 7 (5), then 8 (4). YouTube views=4. The protocol has the lowest mean in Mood & Energy. Panel divided: one sees this as a failure, another sees it as a signal of threshold effects.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: For a mood boost, this track is hit-or-miss — some find it uplifting, others don’t feel it.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=17) shows 9/17 'About 5 minutes' settle time, 6 'Immediately.' Music split (8 'Liked,' 5 'Neutral'). The distribution shows a bimodal pattern: 4 at 4, 4 at 9. YouTube views=5. The protocol is the only one with a polarized response. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 — high. Panel divided: one sees this as a design flaw, another sees it as a sign of strong response for certain audiences.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: For motivation, this track gently lifts the weight, helping some feel ready to move.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 (developing). Qualitative (n=21) shows 5/21 'About 20 minutes' settle time, 6 'About 10 minutes.' Music opinion: 9 'Loved it.' The protocol has the lowest pct_pos (43%). YouTube views=7. The data suggests a low uptake. Panel divided: one model sees this as a design failure, another sees it as an early-phase signal.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: A steady build in drive, this track supports those needing a quiet push forward.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 (developing). Qualitative (n=17) shows 5/17 'About 5 minutes,' 4 'Immediately.' The distribution is bimodal: 4 in score 0–3, 8 in 7–10. YouTube views=7. The protocol has the highest std in TRANSFORMING. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, above observed — same as X-MD-L1. Cross-run drift at 0.507 — the lowest. Panel strongly divided: one sees this as a powerful dual-state protocol, another sees it as fundamentally unbalanced.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol can spark a strong surge in motivation — but only for some listeners.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=25) shows 9 'Loved it,' 8 'Very settling' — the strongest music/rhythm reception in TRANSFORMING. But 2 report 'It actually made things worse.' The distribution is flat. YouTube views=8. Panel divided: one model sees this as high-risk/high-reward, another sees it as a cohort effect.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: A focused guide for mental clarity, it helps many feel sharper and more present.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 (developing). Qualitative (n=20) shows 6 'Loved it,' 12 'Very settling' — the cleanest feedback in TRANSFORMING. The protocol is highly liked. YouTube views=12. No AutoGluon signal. Panel divided on whether this is a design win or a population skew.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: A clean, intentional track that helps clear mental noise and boost awareness.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45 (developing). Qualitative (n=17) shows 5 'No effect,' 3 'Slight improvement.' The distribution is sparse and flat. Rhythm is rated 'Too busy' at 4/17. YouTube views=8. The protocol has the lowest mean and highest variance. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 — the highest of any X-MC — suggesting a strong design signal misaligned with current reception. Panel deeply divided: one sees it as a failure, another sees it as an early-stage bimodal test.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This track is still finding its way — some find it helpful, others feel it lacks clarity.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The Drum Protocols dataset shows strong aggregate efficacy across all three series, with HEALING and THRIVING demonstrating near-identical mean scores (~6.97) and low variance, while TRANSFORMING shows lower means and higher spread as expected for its ascending-arc design. AutoGluon identifies engagement metrics as the strongest outcome predictors, and cross-bucket routing reveals unexpected overlaps between protocol zones. Most protocols have very low YouTube views, suggesting strong efficacy is not yet reaching its target audience.
• HEALING shows high consistency with minimal variance, supporting its regulation-focused design.
• THRIVING achieves high mean scores, especially in Focus & Clarity, but with uneven progression across levels.
• TRANSFORMING's high variance reflects true population heterogeneity in low-affect state experiences.
• AutoGluon cross-bucket signals suggest Mood & Energy protocols may be effective across multiple zones.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The series reflect their intended design profiles with minimal deviation from expectations.
• HEALING leads in reliability and volume, with tight distributions across anxiety, crisis, and overstimulation protocols.
• THRIVING shows the highest mean, driven primarily by the top-level T-FC-L3 (Summit), but other protocols plateau in the mid-6s.
• TRANSFORMING carries the highest variance and lowest mean across its categories, consistent with its ascending-arc intent and self-selecting audience.
• Both AutoGluon and qualitative data confirm that THRIVING’s HOLD protocols are experiencing differential efficacy by intent.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocoI-level patterns deviate from expected norms and warrant targeted investigation.
• T-FC-L3 (Summit) shows a bimodal distribution with a strong spike at 10 and a secondary cluster at 5-7 — a sign of responder/non-responder split.
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) achieves the lowest mean (5.7) with the highest trans-series std (2.45) and a sparse distribution — potentially a cohort mismatch.
• H-CR-L1 reports a ceiling in scores (max 8) despite high qualitative improvement — may reflect caregiver reporting bias.
• AutoGluon cross-bucket signals repeatedly route X-ME-L1 and X-ME-L2 to non-Mood & Energy zones — a robust signal of cross-zone efficacy.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Multiple analyzers converge on development signals, indicating high-confidence next steps.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1: Extend the hold phase to sustain entrainment, given AutoGluon predicts a lower score (6.859) than observed (7.15).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10): Create an L2 variant with simplified rhythm to test whether complexity contributes to the high variance and low mean.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1: Adjust metadata and description to reflect its cross-bucket routing potential — current branding may inhibit adoption in non-mood contexts.
• H-CR-L2: Investigate source split (adviser-driven vs unknown) as AutoGluon flags a large predicted-vs-observed gap (7.955 vs 6.52).
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals confirm both stability and instability in analytical framing.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 22 of 30 protocols show similarity <0.7, with X-MD-L3 (0.507) and H-SL-L1 (0.530) as the most divergent — indicating substantial evolution in analytical interpretation over time.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-FC-L1 ↔ T-FC-L2 (0.878) and H-SL-L1 ↔ H-SL-L2 (0.869) form tight families, while T-FC-L3 and H-OS-L3 are analytically isolated — suggesting distinct response profiles.
• Confidence note: Drift data is robust, but clustering relies on a single run; longitudinal confirmation awaits more data.

The distribution is steep-front-loaded: 17/18 responses at 7–10. YouTube views=7. The protocol is the highest in Creative Flow but used in sleep contexts (5/25), suggesting functional overreach. Panel divided on whether this is a design strength or misalignment.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=23) reports 9/23 'Slightly busy' rhythm, the worst in THRIVING. Distribution bimodal, peaking at 7 (12) and tailing at 4 (6). AutoGluon no signal on rhythm feature. Panel divided: one model sees rhythm as overwhelming, another finds it consistent with focus design.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=17) shows 9/17 'About 10 minutes' for settle time. The distribution peaks at 8 (10) — the strongest in the series. Music opinion split (8 'Liked,' 5 'Loved'), rhythm 'Balanced' (8). The protocol shows the largest L1→L2 jump in THRIVING. Panel divided on whether this is a feature gradient or groove effect.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=29) shows 11/29 'Significant improvement,' 7 'Noticeable.' The distribution is bimodal: peaks at 7 (7) and 10 (5). YouTube views=15. The protocol has the highest mean but also highest variance. AutoGluon predicts 7.728. Panel deeply divided: one sees this as a success of the HOLD gradient, another sees it as a responder/non-responder split.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=23) reports 9/23 'About 20 minutes' settle time — the longest in THRIVING. Music opinions split. Distribution is balanced: 7 at 7 and 8. No AutoGluon signal. Panel divided: some see this as intentional, others see it as slow for maintenance.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=16) shows 7/16 'Slightly busy' rhythm — same as T-FC-L1. The distribution is messy, flat, with a 0-score. YouTube views=4. The protocol is the weakest in the series. Panel divided on whether this is a design fail or cohort mismatch.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73 (developing). Qualitative (n=19) shows 4/19 'Did NOT settle' — the strongest failure-to-settle signal in THRIVING. Music opinion mixed. The protocol is analytically isolated. Panel divided: one model sees this as a population split, another sees it as a usability flaw.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=25) shows 9 'Loved it,' 8 'Very settling' — the strongest music/rhythm reception in TRANSFORMING. But 2 report 'It actually made things worse.' The distribution is flat. YouTube views=8. Panel divided: one model sees this as high-risk/high-reward, another sees it as a cohort effect.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 (developing). Qualitative (n=20) shows 6 'Loved it,' 12 'Very settling' — the cleanest feedback in TRANSFORMING. The protocol is highly liked. YouTube views=12. No AutoGluon signal. Panel divided on whether this is a design win or a population skew.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45 (developing). Qualitative (n=17) shows 5 'No effect,' 3 'Slight improvement.' The distribution is sparse and flat. Rhythm is rated 'Too busy' at 4/17. YouTube views=8. The protocol has the lowest mean and highest variance. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 — the highest of any X-MC — suggesting a strong design signal misaligned with current reception. Panel deeply divided: one sees it as a failure, another sees it as an early-stage bimodal test.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=17) shows 9/17 'About 5 minutes' settle time, 6 'Immediately.' Music split (8 'Liked,' 5 'Neutral'). The distribution shows a bimodal pattern: 4 at 4, 4 at 9. YouTube views=5. The protocol is the only one with a polarized response. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 — high. Panel divided: one sees this as a design flaw, another sees it as a sign of strong response for certain audiences.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 (developing). Qualitative (n=21) shows 5/21 'About 20 minutes' settle time, 6 'About 10 minutes.' Music opinion: 9 'Loved it.' The protocol has the lowest pct_pos (43%). YouTube views=7. The data suggests a low uptake. Panel divided: one model sees this as a design failure, another sees it as an early-phase signal.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 (developing). Qualitative (n=17) shows 5/17 'About 5 minutes,' 4 'Immediately.' The distribution is bimodal: 4 in score 0–3, 8 in 7–10. YouTube views=7. The protocol has the highest std in TRANSFORMING. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, above observed — same as X-MD-L1. Cross-run drift at 0.507 — the lowest. Panel strongly divided: one sees this as a powerful dual-state protocol, another sees it as fundamentally unbalanced.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=22) shows 6 'About 20 minutes' settle time, 6 'Immediately.' Music: 10 'Loved,' 6 'Neutral.' AutoGluon predicts 8.230 — the highest of all zones, over 1.4 points above observed. Cross-buckets to anxiety, crisis, low, and focus. The protocol is the top cross-zone signal. Panel divided: one sees this as a meta-protocol, another sees it as underperforming.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 (meaningful confidence). Qualitative (n=15) shows bimodal settle time: 4 'About 20 minutes,' 4 'Immediately.' Music: 7 'Loved.' AutoGluon predicts 8.230 — same as X-ME-L1. Cross-buckets to anxiety, crisis, low, and focus. The model sees this as a top-tier protocol despite modest outcome. Panel divided: one sees it as a strong design, another sees it as a signal gap.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 (developing). Qualitative (n=25) shows 5 'No effect,' 3 'Slight improvement.' The distribution shows a flat peak at 7 (5), then 8 (4). YouTube views=4. The protocol has the lowest mean in Mood & Energy. Panel divided: one sees this as a failure, another sees it as a signal of threshold effects.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: A calming track that helps many anxious listeners decompress, with most feeling noticeably better after a short settling period.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A gentle way to ease anxiety, offering steady relief without dramatic changes — ideal for daily resilience.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: A consistent ally for stress, this track gently guides listeners toward a calmer, clearer state.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: In a moment of crisis, this track brings quick relief, helping many feel steadier within minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: This protocol helps steady the ship during acute distress, offering reliable relief for most users.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Designed for those in deep distress, it helps some settle quickly and find a sense of calm.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: This track cuts through sensory overload with clarity, helping many find calm again.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: For overstimulation, this offers a balanced, gentle release that many find stabilizing.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: A refined tool for sensory overload, it brings quiet focus, especially useful before sleep.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: A powerful sleep aid, this track helps many fall asleep faster and feel more refreshed.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: A subtle but effective choice for rest, this protocol promotes relaxation with gentle cues.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: A quiet companion for winding down, most users feel its calming effect by the end.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: For creative work, this track gently clears mental blocks and invites flow.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: As your creative session begins, this track helps sharpen focus and spark ideas.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: At peak creative moments, this track amplifies clarity and unlocks deeper expression.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: A focused tool for clarity, it helps steady the mind and sharpen attention.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: This track helps you lock in, especially when concentration is vital for the task.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: For deep focus, this protocol enhances mental clarity and keeps distractions at bay.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: A consistent anchor, this protocol helps maintain balance through daily challenges.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: A steady presence, it offers support when balance feels out of reach.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: This track gently brings clarity to mental fog, grounding you when you need it.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: A focused guide for mental clarity, it helps many feel sharper and more present.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: A clean, intentional track that helps clear mental noise and boost awareness.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: This track is still finding its way — some find it helpful, others feel it lacks clarity.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: For motivation, this track gently lifts the weight, helping some feel ready to move.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: A steady build in drive, this track supports those needing a quiet push forward.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: This protocol can spark a strong surge in motivation — but only for some listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: This track lifts mood and energy, helping some feel more alive and present.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: A gentle boost in energy and mood, it supports those needing a quiet lift.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: For a mood boost, this track is hit-or-miss — some find it uplifting, others don’t feel it.
